meaning: v.以策略制胜,以机动性胜过
examples: He said the boat was trying to outmaneuver the Israeli vessel when it was struck.
He say the boat were trying to outmaneuver the Israeli vessel when it were struck.
Wardens have a line of sight teleport ability called "blink," which they can use to outmaneuver even the quickest of foes.
Of the firms who did outmaneuver the markets, no firm held the top spot for the entire period.
More recently, Lilly, which has an entire department devoted exclusively to managing alliances, was able to outmaneuver Bristol-Myers Squibb Co.
